grant_title: United Way of Southern Maine Annual Giving
geographic_restrictions: Southern Maine, specifically York County and Cumberland County (excluding Brunswick and Harpswell)
project_area: Educational success and youth opportunity, financial security and stability, health and well-being, child care access, food insecurity, housing instability, mental health services, community resilience, racial equity initiatives
demographic_requirements: Must serve residents of York or Cumberland County (excluding Brunswick and Harpswell) and endorse Thrive2027's three ten-year goals (Kids getting a strong start, Neighbors empowered to thrive, People living longer, better lives)
eligibility_summary: Organizations must request a minimum of $10,000, serve residents of York or Cumberland County (excluding Brunswick and Harpswell), endorse Thrive2027's goals, and complete a volunteer-led review process. Does not fund general operating expenses, capital campaigns, previously incurred expenses, religious activities, or political lobbying.
